Supplementary data & analysis code

Higher exercise intensity and working memory: a reversed digit-span study (within-subject, N = 14)
Drechsel, Philipps, Jägeler & Heuschling · German Sport University Cologne

This page hosts the raw data files and the R analysis script as plain text. Use the buttons to copy or download each file.

analyze_digit_span_hrr.R

R analysis script · 163 lines · 8 KB

## =============================================================================
## Beeinflusst die objektive Belastung (%HRR) die Leistung im Reversed-
## Digit-Span Test?
##
## H1: Es existiert ein Unterschied in der Leistung zwischen den unter-
##     schiedlichen Belastungsstufen und Ruhe.
##
## Design: within-subject / repeated measures.
##         Each participant (n = 14) completed the reversed digit span task
##         once at rest ("Ruhe") and once at each of three relative exercise
##         intensities (50%, 75%, 85% heart-rate-reserve, HFR).
##
## Primary DV:  total_score   (number of correctly recalled sequences,
##                              one value per participant x condition)
## Secondary DV: max_span     (longest span recalled correctly)
## =============================================================================

## ---- 0. Packages -----------------------------------------------------------
required_pkgs <- c("tidyverse", "rstatix", "ggpubr", "lme4", "lmerTest", "emmeans")
new_pkgs <- required_pkgs[!(required_pkgs %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"])]
if (length(new_pkgs)) install.packages(new_pkgs, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")

library(tidyverse)
library(rstatix)   # tidy ANOVA / post-hoc for repeated measures
library(ggpubr)    # publication-ready plots
library(lme4)      # mixed model (robustness check)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)   # contrasts for the mixed model

## ---- 1. Load & clean data ---------------------------------------------------
digit_raw <- read_csv("df_digit.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  select(-1) |>                                  # drop leftover index column
  mutate(
    participant_id = toupper(participant_id),     # "vp04"/"VP04" -> same person
    condition = str_trim(condition)
  )

## Order conditions from rest -> increasing intensity, and add a simple
## two-level grouping (rest vs. any exercise) to test the hypothesis as stated
digit_raw <- digit_raw |>
  mutate(
    condition = factor(condition,
                        levels = c("Ruhe", "50% HFR", "75% HFR", "85% HFR"),
                        labels = c("Rest", "50% HRR", "75% HRR", "85% HRR")),
    intensity_group = if_else(condition == "Rest", "Rest", "Exercise") |>
      factor(levels = c("Rest", "Exercise"))
  )

## Each trial row repeats the session-level outcome (total_score, max_span)
## 14x per participant x condition -> collapse to one row per cell
digit <- digit_raw |>
  group_by(participant_id, condition, intensity_group) |>
  summarise(
    total_score = first(total_score),
    max_span    = first(max_span),
    mean_rpe    = mean(rpe_borg_6_20, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(participant_id = factor(participant_id))

cat("\n--- Sample sizes per condition ---\n")
print(digit |> count(condition))

cat("\n--- Descriptive statistics: total_score ---\n")
print(digit |> group_by(condition) |> get_summary_stats(total_score, type = "mean_sd"))

## ---- 2. Visual check --------------------------------------------------------
p_box <- ggboxplot(digit, x = "condition", y = "total_score",
                    add = "jitter", fill = "condition", alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(title = "Reversed digit span performance by intensity condition",
       x = "Condition", y = "Total score (correct sequences)") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
ggsave("digit_span_by_condition.png", p_box, width = 6, height = 4.5, dpi = 150)

## ---- 3. Check assumptions ----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n--- Normality of total_score within each condition (Shapiro-Wilk) ---\n")
print(digit |> group_by(condition) |> shapiro_test(total_score))

## ---- 4. Omnibus test: repeated-measures ANOVA across all 4 conditions ------
## (Tests whether performance differs across rest / 50% / 75% / 85% HRR at all.
##  rstatix automatically reports the Greenhouse-Geisser corrected p-value
##  if sphericity is violated, via Mauchly's test below.)
cat("\n--- Mauchly's test of sphericity ---\n")
rm_aov <- anova_test(data = digit, dv = total_score, wid = participant_id,
                      within = condition, detailed = TRUE)
print(get_anova_table(rm_aov))  # auto GG-correction applied if needed

## ---- 5. Direct test of the stated hypothesis ---------------------------------
## H1: performance differs between REST and the (collapsed) EXERCISE conditions
cat("\n=== Primary hypothesis test: Rest vs. Exercise (collapsed across intensities) ===\n")

wide_group <- digit |>
  group_by(participant_id, intensity_group) |>
  summarise(total_score = mean(total_score), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = intensity_group, values_from = total_score)

## Normality of the paired differences
diff_scores <- wide_group$Exercise - wide_group$Rest
cat("\nShapiro-Wilk test on paired differences (Exercise - Rest):\n")
print(shapiro.test(diff_scores))

## Paired t-test (parametric)
ttest_result <- t.test(wide_group$Exercise, wide_group$Rest, paired = TRUE)
cat("\nPaired t-test (Exercise vs Rest):\n")
print(ttest_result)

## Effect size (Cohen's d for paired samples)
d_paired <- digit |>
  group_by(participant_id, intensity_group) |>
  summarise(total_score = mean(total_score), .groups = "drop") |>
  cohens_d(total_score ~ intensity_group, paired = TRUE)
cat("\nEffect size (Cohen's d, paired):\n")
print(d_paired)

## Non-parametric backup (Wilcoxon signed-rank), useful given small n = 14
cat("\nWilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric backup):\n")
print(wilcox.test(wide_group$Exercise, wide_group$Rest, paired = TRUE))

## ---- 6. Pairwise post-hoc: each intensity vs. Rest ---------------------------
cat("\n=== Post-hoc: each exercise intensity vs. Rest (paired t-tests, Holm-corrected) ===\n")
posthoc <- digit |>
  pairwise_t_test(total_score ~ condition, paired = TRUE, p.adjust.method = "holm") |>
  filter(group1 == "Rest" | group2 == "Rest")
print(posthoc)

## ---- 7. Robustness check: mixed-effects model on raw trial-level data --------
## Treats participant as a random intercept; condition as a within-subject fixed
## effect. Uses the binary per-trial accuracy outcome (logistic mixed model),
## which avoids the sphericity assumption and uses the data at its native
## (trial-level, unaggregated) grain as a cross-check on the ANOVA above.
## accuracy (0/1 per trial) -> logistic mixed model, participant as random intercept
cat("\n=== Robustness check: logistic mixed model on trial-level accuracy ===\n")
mm_data <- digit_raw |>
  mutate(condition = relevel(condition, ref = "Rest"))

mixed_model <- glmer(accuracy ~ condition + (1 | participant_id),
                      data = mm_data, family = binomial)
print(summary(mixed_model))

cat("\nEstimated marginal means (log-odds) & contrasts vs Rest:\n")
print(emmeans(mixed_model, specs = "condition") |> contrast(method = "trt.vs.ctrl"))

## ---- 8. Secondary outcome: max_span (same logic) ------------------------------
cat("\n=== Secondary outcome: max_span, Rest vs Exercise ===\n")
wide_group_span <- digit |>
  group_by(participant_id, intensity_group) |>
  summarise(max_span = mean(max_span), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = intensity_group, values_from = max_span)
print(t.test(wide_group_span$Exercise, wide_group_span$Rest, paired = TRUE))

## ---- 9. Summary printout -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=============================================================\n")
cat("SUMMARY\n")
cat("=============================================================\n")
cat(sprintf("Rest mean total_score:     %.2f (SD %.2f)\n",
            mean(wide_group$Rest), sd(wide_group$Rest)))
cat(sprintf("Exercise mean total_score: %.2f (SD %.2f)\n",
            mean(wide_group$Exercise), sd(wide_group$Exercise)))
cat(sprintf("Paired t-test: t(%d) = %.2f, p = %.4f\n",
            ttest_result$parameter, ttest_result$statistic, ttest_result$p.value))
cat("See console output above for the omnibus RM-ANOVA, post-hoc comparisons\n")
cat("per intensity level, and the linear mixed-model robustness check.\n")
